name: SymPy (Symbolic Mathematics)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD 3-Clause); pure Python symbolic math library

performance.notes: Pure Python; complex symbolic computations can be slow; simplification is NP-hard in general

agent_readiness.notes: Pure Python symbolic mathematics library. Symbolic computation: algebra, calculus, differential equations, matrices, number theory, combinatorics, logic, geometry, physics, statistics. LaTeX output. Code generation (C, Fortran, Python, Julia, JavaScript). Used in AI math reasoning, physics simulations, equation solving. Integrates with Jupyter. Can solve systems of equations, differentiate/integrate symbolically, prove identities. Much used in AI math competition systems.

gotchas: Slow for complex expressions (pure Python, not C-backed like Mathematica)
Simplification not always optimal (simplest form is undecidable)
May not solve all equations (especially transcendental)
Expression comparison requires .equals() not == in some contexts
best_when: AI math reasoning systems needing symbolic computation
Calculus, algebra, and differential equation solving
avoid_when: Complex CAS needing full Mathematica/Maple capabilities
summary: SymPy is Python's symbolic mathematics library. Handles algebra, calculus, differential equations, matrices, number theory, and logic. LaTeX output. Code generation to C/Fortran/Python/Julia. Used in AI math reasoning systems, physics simulations, and equation verification. Pure Python (slower than Mathematica) but free and integrates with the Python ecosystem.
